content:
<:Tulu_sleep2:1468259861598044402> 
Time to yapp about the game specifically:

I love both the male and female designs, each one having a random apendage or just has their guts out (Uvhash)
Ive grown very numb to just "pretty woman with big boobs" (and even some men designs)

It's not every day you see a kid whose insides are their fluff, a jellyfish girl.
Even if I knew nothing of the cosmic beings, I can still appreciate these character designs.
There are some weaker ones like Leigh. Wish we got a dreamdive to see her past one day, maybe see her brother.

About stuff outside the gameplay (it's good I got nothing to really add to It)

I really appreciate the covenant system (artefacts) being able to just reuse the same set on multiple teams without locking you out in D-zone is very freeing. Even if I can make one really good set about once a month. Not having a "grind this dungeon for better piece" is welcomed.

The dupe system to many is a dud but to me atleast it doesn't bother me since you are able to accumulate a good amount of resources. I mean it's a Gacha game, somewhere it has to be stingy like it or not. And hell, standard units are still pulling their weight and some are even reinvigorated with newer releases (Liz).

I wish the exploration would return to Morimens in story. Some dislike it but others like it too.

I GREATLY appreciate that I am able to sweep events and other stuff that has been cleared. Very time saving. Tho it does bother me that some players don't like when time sensitive event content is added (the current STS mode) with their issue being... Play the game a bit. Branching paths can be the next thing to be added. And a sell/destroy function for relics in that mode.


To the team. Thanks for making and maintaining such a unique game. Specially thanks to the music person, also please add more of the ost to YouTube, thank god SeaLily is uploading it, but it would be nice to have the official source too <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>

Realize I did not answer these questions:

1. Started ever since the game released on Steam.

2. The scene that touched me the most, or rather, what made me laugh harder than I should have is how ChatGPT translated Erica's posse story since it translated Tick-Tock Man as the social media Tik-Tok.The part where her line said "I'm sorry Tik-Tok People... In the end, I have to destroy you myself" destroyed my lungs

3. Arachne update. I love Atlach Nacha.

4. I knew about the situation. It didnt know yall struggles at 2021 as well.

5. Survive!

1. Started a couple months before Doresain. Someone on another gacha discord saw me discussion Call of Cthulhu and said "You'd probably like Morimens." I did.

2. Thais's chapter captured a lot of themes of cosmic horror, including some adjacent to body horror that don't get brought up much for obvious reasons. So I was very happy to see that side of a Lovecraftian entity brought up. Also, at the risk of spoilers, nice to see something of a Bad Ending occur with Remouahe; it wouldn't feel like cosmic horror if we got a fairy tale ending like most gacha game arcs.

3. As with every gacha game, I really hope to see more story for existing characters and even NPCs like Hannah over constantly moving towards new people and new settings. Though I'm sure we'll get more on the Panlong Empire. I would like to see Mythag developed more specifically: the layout of the campus, what they actually do there, etc. 

4. I do enjoy the behind the scenes stuff. As a long-time Call of Cthulhu GM and cosmic horror writer, it's great to see what creative people like and make with these themes.

5. I hope the team knows their work is enjoyed, and even when people are complaining about balance, a certain amount of that is just people being excited to use their favorite characters more. I look forward to see more designs, more atmospheric music, and most of all more stories to explore.

1. Came across the game on Steam either in the Free-to-Play section halfway down the Steam page or that one part of Steam if you scroll down enough you'll see a game and a review someone gave (I dont remember which one or what way i found this game) and wanted to give the game a try
2. The one moment that really made me want to continue playing the game was when Aigis dissoluted in the first chapter and I was so shocked, like "Damn, they're really not afraid of making even a child die" in such a poetic way with a very good boss soundtrack
3. Men. I want more men in the game. Sad that despite the name "Mori**mens**" there really isn't a lot of men. Jokes aside, the character and kit designs are for the most part all very well done across the whole roster. I'm mostly excited for the character designs for those thay will come next and more combat event varieties, it feels a bit repetitive.
4. I don't really follow the plot for behind-the-scene stories. I probably should spend a little more time on them.
5. Great job on surviving this precarious environment. I'd spend on the game if i could. I'm glad I decided to give this game I randomly found on Steam a chance. 大家加油

I started playing in early 2025.
I really loved the part where sylvester thought if his actions were worth it and I loved Ramona's encouragement.
I really am excited for the main story. It is just soo interesting and gripping.
I really am glad that you decided to share this. it really puts in perspective what is happening with all of y'all.
Thanks for the amazing story both in game and in real life!

**1. When did you start playing Morimens, and what brought you here?**

It was in the middle of summer when I finally lend my hands on Morimens because I saw lots of splash arts on Pinterest iirc and decided to give it a try. Kinda embarassing to say that I visually and aesthetically adore characters with disabilities more and when I saw Genesis Ramona with her blindfold and Alistar on wheelchair (at first I thought he was a female xD) I finally set my mind on, oh and Leigh`s splash art got me really captivated. Now that I think about it more - it is actually the unique art-style that got me on while GRamona is my light that keeps me going!

**2. Which scene in the game touched you the most?**

I barely cry over anything but the scene where Sylvester had to break the link with all the Awakeners and say goodbye to Ramona...I was literally in tears during that scene (actually during the whole Chapter 8 and 9). This happened again when Sylvester and Ramona managed to meet again but had to say goodbye to each other one more time and I was teared up again...

**3. What kind of content updates are you most excited to see?**

Honestly Im fine with everything B.I.A.V. team is doing! I mean the new rogue-lite event is hella fun and I hope there will be more improvements to that oh and I want more OSTs on Spotify badly...

**4. Do you enjoy these behind-the-scenes stories? Who else would you like to hear about?**

Absolutely! I would love to hear more about music production and the process behind concept-designing, these are very enjoyable!

**5. Is there anything you want to say to our team?**

Thank you so much! I have never experienced a game that got me in tears twice, and that has the most touching and emotional soundtrack I have ever heard. I love everything about Morimens: characters, music, world-building, gameplay, art-style, voice acting and devs!

Tell him I love Cassiah's theme! It's got the perfect "carnival/magic show" kind of feel to it while still sounding like suitable battle music and not sounding derivative of the more stereotypical carnival music songs

We absolutely love bringing new AAs (Alternate Arts) to our Awakeners! 
However, since our current roster is still relatively small, we're prioritizing our production capacity on new characters and Genesis forms first to expand the world. But rest assured, we hope to push forward with more AA content as soon as possible!

你说得对——游戏体验永远是第一位的。
我们也深知行动胜于言语。写这些文字是我复盘和与大家沟通的一种方式，包括此前有玩家提议希望看到分享徐的趣闻，所以需要兑现。只不过越写越觉得意义深远，就将过去的故事一起写下来了。这次系统性的写出来之后，我们就会尽量再少提及过去的种种。
我们同时也正在全力优化当前版本的BUG和细节，并推进新内容。感谢你的鞭策。<:ptincess:1356053126247092476>
猫咪猫咪所以这游戏的男女角色比例到底是什么 。

1. I started playing Morimens in the summer of 2025.
All it took was a few images. I saw some character art and enemy designs, and something inside me immediately clicked. I downloaded the game without hesitation.
Morimens is honestly one of the most beautiful games I have ever seen. Not just visually, but emotionally. The art, the music, the atmosphere… everything. I constantly show it to my friends and tell them how stunning it is. I never get tired of talking about it.
This is the first gacha game in my life where I genuinely wanted to spend money. Not because I had to, not because of pressure, but because I wanted to say **thank you**. The game is generous, yes, but my support comes from gratitude. I want this project to live. I want it to grow. Supporting it feels personal to me.
I want to be part of Morimens in every possible way. Part of the community, part of its future. I work in digital marketing, and if you ever needed someone who truly loves this game and believes in it deeply, I would happily help. I mean it when I say I would work just for pictures of my favorite characters. х)) 
And please… I would buy every single piece of merch if I could. I miss having something physical to hold that connects me to this world.

2. Chapter 8 and 9.
Watching Leonora break up with the Awakeners was almost physically painful for me. I did not expect a game to make me feel that way. By that point, they were not just characters. They felt like family. Each one of them.
Losing them felt real. I cried. Not a little emotional moment. I was genuinely in tears.
The only thing that comforted me was knowing the game continues. That there is a Astral Reign. That hope still exists in this world. That warmth of hope means so much to me.
I also love all the individual character stories. Their backgrounds, their tiny personality details. When I found out that I share a birthday with Xu, I was ridiculously happy!!! It felt like such a small thing, but it made me smile all day. ❤️
Will the team continue to release characters every month forever or is there already a plan on how many characters the game will end up with (not including re releases like genesis)

Thank you for loving Ramona's voice acting! Ai Yamamoto is absolutely our MVP ! 

She single-handedly voiced Ramona, Ramona: Timeworn, Thias, AND Tawil... talk about range! <:ramona_wisdom:1468258669341966452> <:blind:1356053033724674255> <:bow:1356053037159944473> <:Tawil_loneliness:1460158738110349372> 

And we're so glad you appreciate Faros! I'll definitely pass your voice along to the creative team—more eldritch designs incoming! <:Faros_Target:1468259023282503712>

**3. What kind of content updates are you most excited to see?**
I genuinely enjoy almost everything. Events, story chapters, banners. But the communicator messages from the Awakeners are something very special to me.
Those private messages felt so intimate. So personal. It really felt like they were writing to me. I would read them slowly, sometimes re-read them, just to feel that connection again.

Now that I have almost all characters, there are fewer new messages, and I actually miss them. They created such a strong emotional closeness.

I also want to mention Reproductive Frenzy. I open those stories with my heart racing. There is anticipation, excitement, almost a physical tension. It feels intense in the best possible way. That kind of emotional storytelling is unforgettable.

**4. Do you enjoy these behind-the-scenes stories? Who else would you like to hear about?**
Yes. Very much.
Behind-the-scenes stories make me feel even closer to the project. I love knowing how characters were designed, how music was composed, how ideas were born. I could honestly listen to that forever.

It makes everything feel human. It reminds me there are real people behind this world, pouring their feelings into it.
Please, share more.
This message genuinely warmed our hearts. Hearing this is the best motivation any developer could ask for. We've received your feedback! Thank you! <:Pandia_love:1459078512303411261>
